seoTitle: Edit Your Chart of Accounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Rename, restructure, or merge accounts in your QuickBooks Online Chart of Accounts without breaking your historical reports.
intro: As a business grows, its Chart of Accounts often needs cleanup, whether that's renaming categories, nesting sub-accounts, or merging duplicates. This guide shows how to edit your Chart of Accounts in QuickBooks Online safely.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Go to Accounting, then Chart of Accounts, to see your full ledger list.

steps.heading: Find the account to edit
steps.body: Scroll or search to locate the specific category you want to change.

steps.heading: Open the edit drawer
steps.body: Click the action dropdown arrow next to the target row and select Edit.

steps.heading: Update the details
steps.body: Rewrite the account name and description, and check or uncheck Is Sub-Account to adjust hierarchy.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save and Close, then run a Trial Balance report to confirm your changes didn't disrupt ledger consistency.

faq.question: Can I merge two duplicate accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, editing one account's name to exactly match another triggers a merge prompt that consolidates both and remaps historical transactions.

faq.question: Will renaming an account affect past reports?
faq.answer: The name updates across your entire reporting framework, but historical transaction amounts remain unchanged.

faq.question: How do I nest an account under another one?
faq.answer: Check the Is Sub-Account box in the edit drawer and select the parent account you want it nested under.

faq.question: What should I check after editing accounts?
faq.answer: Run a quick Trial Balance report right after editing to verify your ledger is still consistent.
